author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
sandarśana, saṁsparśana, jalpa, vartma-nirodhana,
rāsa, vṛndāvana⋅līlā bhūri
jala-keli jamunāya, naukā-khelā, cauryatāya,
ghaṭṭa⋅līlā, kuñje lukocuri

madhupāna, vadhūveṣa, kapaṭa⋅nidrā⋅āveśa,
dyūtakrīḍā, vastra⋅ṭānāṭāni
cumbāśleṣa, nakhārpaṇa, bimbādhara⋅sudhāpāna,
samprayoga ādi līlā māni

sambhogaprakāra saba, sambhogera mahotsava,
līlā haya sadā supeśala
sei līlā aparūpa, ujjvala rasera kūpa,
tāhe jāra haya kautūhala

cidvilāsa⋅rasabhare, rati bhāva⋅daśā dhare,
mahābhāva paryanta bāḍaya
je jīva saubhāgyavān, līlā-yoge su-sandhāna,
vraje vasi’ satata karaya
en_translation:
Sandarśana, saṁsparśana, jalpa, vartmanirodhana, the rāsa, and the abundant pastimes of Vṛndāvana—water sports in the Yamunā, boat playing, stealing, pastimes on the river bank, and hide-and-seek in the groves.

Madhupāna, vadhūveṣa, feigned sleep, gambling, pulling at clothes, kissing and embracing, nail marks, and drinking the nectar of bimba-like lips—I consider these and other intimate pastimes.

All types of union and the great festival of enjoyment, the pastimes are always delightful. Those unique pastimes are a well of brilliant rasa; for one who has a keen interest in them,

filled with the mellow of spiritual enjoyment, rati attains the stage of bhāva and increases up to mahābhāva. That fortunate soul, through connection with these pastimes, constantly resides in Vraja and performs them.
